candidate_summary: Reframe LLM alignment 'narrow safety basin' (AsFT) via pyriform-egg cliff-ledge stability: stability comes from long contact-ridge friction, not narrow roll-arc. Proposes null-space-traversal that projects updates perpendicular to or along a contact-ridge tangent depending on proximity to the safety boundary.

results.title: The pyriform egg of the Common Murre is more stable on sloping surfaces (Auk 2018)
results.snippet: Common Murre eggs more stable than Razorbill; more pyriform => more stable on slope; selection favors pyriform shape on slope.
results.judge_reason: Source-domain biology paper on pyriform-egg slope stability; no functional LLM alignment content.

results.title: The point of a Guillemot's egg (BOU, Birkhead)
results.snippet: Roll-arc of pyriform egg is wider than most cliff ledges so not roll-stop; stability from long straight contact-edge increasing friction.
results.judge_reason: Birkhead source itself; directly the analog source but not LLM-side equivalence.

results.title: Egg shape in Common Guillemot Uria aalge and Brunnich's Guillemot U. lomvia: not a rolling matter?
results.snippet: Source-domain study of pyriform-egg evolutionary stability.
results.judge_reason: Pure source-domain ornithology with no LLM relevance.

results.title: Geometry-Aligned LLM Fine-Tuning for Sequential Narrow-Opening Planning
results.snippet: Failure-driven training loop with deterministic geometric verifier returning failing waypoint indices and violation types (boundary, collision, swept collision, step-size); LoRA-based SFT to keep solutions in workspace.
results.judge_reason: Geometry-aligned fine-tuning with workspace boundary constraints overlaps conceptually with contact-ridge/safety-boundary projection but does not use null-space or tangent decomposition.

results.title: AsFT: Anchoring Safety During LLM Fine-Tuning Within Narrow Safety Basin
results.snippet: Parameter space as narrow safety basin; constrains update directions; penalizes updates orthogonal to alignment direction.
results.judge_reason: AsFT directly constrains update directions in a narrow safety basin during fine-tuning; functionally close inverse of candidate (penalizes orthogonal updates rather than projecting onto null-space).

results.title: SafeConstellations: Steering LLM Safety to Reduce Over-Refusals Through Task-Specific Trajectory
results.snippet: Steer LLM safety with task-specific trajectories to reduce over-refusals.
results.judge_reason: Trajectory-based safety steering differs from the parameter-space null-space/tangent projection mechanism proposed.

results.title: LLM-Enabled Low-Altitude UAV Natural Language Navigation
results.snippet: LLM selects repair modes for constraint conflicts; safety constraints never relaxed.
results.judge_reason: UAV navigation with constraint repair, not parameter-space safety basin or projection.

results.title: A Comprehensive Dataset for Enhancing Safety in LLM (EMNLP 2025)
results.judge_reason: Generic safety dataset paper without mechanism alignment.

verdict: Candidate is partially anticipated: AsFT (idx 4) directly addresses the narrow-safety-basin framing as a near-inverse mechanism, and the Birkhead BOU blog (idx 1) provides the exact source-domain framing for contact-edge friction stability. The specific null-space-vs-tangent state-dependent projection scheme appears not directly published in the retrieved results, so novelty is plausibly preserved but the framing rests on a well-anticipated geometric-safety landscape; net mild novelty risk.
